рџ“° Resource Spotlight: Yojana Magazine

Published by the Government of India вЂ” and directly quoted in UPSC questions.

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

Why Yojana matters:

в†’ Articles written by IAS officers, economists, domain experts
в†’ Every issue covers 8-12 syllabus-aligned themes
в†’ Content appears verbatim in GS and essay questions

How to use it:

рџ“Њ GS2: Deep dives on flagship schemes (Ayushman Bharat, Jal Jeevan Mission) вЂ” directly asked in mains

рџ“Њ GS3: Policy analysis on agriculture, infrastructure, digital India

рџ“Њ Essay: Structure your essays using Yojana's framework вЂ” problem, policy response, impact, way forward

Pro tip: Don't read every article. Pick 3-4 per issue matching your GS gaps. Make one-page summaries.

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

Monthly PDFs free:
https://upscprepnotes.in/free-materials?utm_source=telegram&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=yojana-spotlight

рџ“љ Resource Spotlight: Manorama Yearbook 2026

One book. 11 subjects. Every UPSC aspirant's reference companion.

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

What's inside:
в†’ Structured sections on India's economy, polity, environment, IR
в†’ Updated chapters on G20 outcomes, NEP 2020, Supreme Court judgments 2025

How toppers use it:

рџ“Њ Prelims: Fact sections (awards, appointments, sports, books) = 20-25 GK questions

рџ“Њ Mains: Analytical sections as background for GS answers

рџ“Њ Interview: Who's Who and organizational structure sections

Focus on:
в†’ Charts and infographics (UPSC loves diagram-based questions)
в†’ At a Glance summary boxes
в†’ Comparison tables (India vs World)

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

Free PDF: https://upscprepnotes.in/free-materials/manorama-year-book-2026-v2?utm_source=telegram&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=manorama-spotlight

рџ“ќ Resource Spotlight: Drishti IAS Notes (Bilingual)

The go-to resource for Hindi-medium aspirants вЂ” 40% of UPSC candidates.

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

Why Drishti notes stand out:

в†’ Bilingual presentation вЂ” Hindi and English side-by-side
в†’ 8 subject-wise volumes covering the entire GS syllabus
в†’ Every chapter starts with syllabus demand, then content, PYQs, practice
в†’ Written with the question paper in mind вЂ” no fluff

Best for:
рџ“Њ Prelims: Fact-rich, easy to revise multiple times
рџ“Њ Mains: Structured paragraphs that mirror answer-writing format
рџ“Њ Hindi medium: Parallel English text helps with key terminology

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

Free download:
https://upscprepnotes.in/free-materials/drishti-ias-notes-2025-v2?utm_source=telegram&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=drishti-spotlight

рџ“Љ GS3 is the Rank Decider вЂ” Here's Why

Data from 280+ topper marksheets reveals a clear pattern:

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

рџ“‰ Average GS scores among selected candidates:

GS1: 115
GS2: 116
GS3: 105 в†ђ lowest average
GS4: 117

GS3 has the LOWEST average score across all GS papers. Most candidates вЂ” even those who eventually get rank вЂ” struggle with it.

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

рџ“Њ What this means for you:

в†’ If you score 120+ in GS3, you're already ahead of 80% of selected candidates
в†’ GS3 is where the rank is made or lost вЂ” 15 extra marks here can move your rank by 100+ positions
в†’ Economy + Environment + Agriculture = 60% of the paper. Master these three and you've won half the battle

рџЏ† Top GS3 scorers in database:
в†’ Garima Lohia (AIR 2, 2022) вЂ” scored highest
в†’ Focus on application-based questions, not just theory
в†’ Current affairs linkage is non-negotiable

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

Full paper-wise analysis:
https://upscprepnotes.in/content/how-to-score-120-plus-in-gs3?utm_source=telegram&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=gs3-analysis

рџЋЇ What Marks Do You Need in Each Paper?

Average scores across 280+ toppers (2022-2025):

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

рџ“„ Essay вЂ” Avg: 115 | Max: 149
рџЊЌ GS1 вЂ” Avg: 115 | Max: 148
рџЏ›пёЏ GS2 вЂ” Avg: 116 | Max: 149
рџ“Љ GS3 вЂ” Avg: 105 | Max: 143
вљ–пёЏ GS4 вЂ” Avg: 117 | Max: 141
рџЋЇ Optional вЂ” Avg: 285 | Max: 345
вњЌпёЏ Written Total вЂ” Avg: 872 | Max: 1062
рџЋ¤ Interview вЂ” Avg: 174 | Max: 214

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

рџ“Њ Key insights:

1. GS3 (avg 105) is where ranks are made вЂ” 120+ puts you miles ahead
2. Optional choice can move you 200+ ranks вЂ” Mathematics avg 312 vs Sociology avg 276
3. Interview max variation is only 40 marks вЂ” won't save a bad written
4. Essay is the wildcard вЂ” 149 max vs 115 avg means 34 extra marks are achievable

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

Browse all topper marksheets free:
https://upscprepnotes.in/toppers/marks-database?utm_source=telegram&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=marks-breakdown

рџ“– Essay Writing: The 4-Part Framework Used by 140+ Scorers

Analysis of 50+ topper essay copies reveals a consistent pattern:

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

1пёЏвѓЈ INTRODUCTION (3-4 lines)
в†’ Define the keyword
в†’ Give context (historical/current)
в†’ State your stance

2пёЏвѓЈ BODY (4-6 paragraphs)
в†’ One idea per paragraph
в†’ Use headings, examples, data
в†’ Link to GS subjects naturally

3пёЏвѓЈ COUNTER-ARGUMENT (1 paragraph)
в†’ Acknowledge the other side
в†’ Refute with evidence
в†’ Shows evaluator you've thought critically

4пёЏвѓЈ CONCLUSION (3-4 lines)
в†’ Summarize your argument
в†’ Link to current affairs / vision
в†’ End with a quote or forward-looking statement

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

рџ“Њ Remember: Evaluators read 50+ essays daily. If your essay doesn't flow paragraph-to-paragraph, you hit a 110-120 ceiling regardless of content.

Browse actual topper essay copies:
https://upscprepnotes.in/store/answer-copies-compilation?utm_source=telegram&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=essay-framework

рџ“Љ How Many Toppers Score 130+ in Each GS Paper?

Data from 280+ marksheets:

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

GS1 (130+): 37% of toppers
в†’ Achievable with strong ancient/medieval + society

GS2 (130+): 41% of toppers
в†’ Most predictable paper вЂ” direct syllabus coverage pays off

GS3 (120+): 22% of toppers
в†’ Toughest вЂ” only 1 in 5 selected candidates crosses 120

GS4 (130+): 35% of toppers
в†’ 97% cross 100, but only 1 in 3 crosses 130

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

рџ“Њ The takeaway:

GS3 is the biggest opportunity. Since most candidates score low here, even a moderate improvement gives you a disproportionate rank gain.

If you're averaging 110 in GS3 and push to 125 вЂ” that 15-mark gain is worth more than the same gain in any other paper.

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

Full paper-wise strategies:
https://upscprepnotes.in/free-materials?utm_source=telegram&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=gs-scoring-analysis

рџ”Ќ How to Choose Your Optional Subject вЂ” Data-Backed Approach

Most aspirants choose optionals based on hearsay. Here's what the numbers say:

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

рџ“Љ Average optional marks among toppers:

рџҐ‡ Mathematics вЂ” 312
рџҐ€ Commerce вЂ” 298
рџҐ‰ Anthropology вЂ” 287
4пёЏвѓЈ Geography вЂ” 280
5пёЏвѓЈ Sociology вЂ” 276
6пёЏвѓЈ PSIR вЂ” 268
7пёЏвѓЈ Public Admin вЂ” 264
8пёЏвѓЈ History вЂ” 260
9пёЏвѓЈ Philosophy вЂ” 252
рџ”џ Economics вЂ” 248

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

рџ“Њ But higher avg в‰  easier:

Mathematics has high avg because people who choose it are already strong quantitatively вЂ” it's self-selection, not inherent scoring.

Sociology has lower avg but 40% syllabus overlap with GS вЂ” so you save hundreds of hours.

Ask yourself:
в†’ Do I enjoy the subject or just its scoring trend?
в†’ How much overlap does it have with GS?
в†’ Is study material easily available?
в†’ Can I write 500-word answers in this subject for 3 hours?

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

Detailed optional analysis:
https://upscprepnotes.in/content/upsc-optional-subject-marks-analysis?utm_source=telegram&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=optional-guide
